blocks.task: Monkey compiler: superinstructions (constant-operand opcodes done)
blocks.summary: Superinstructions done.
blocks.details: Superinstructions done. OpGetLocal{Add,Sub,Mul,Div}Const fuses 2 dispatches into 1. Compiler peephole detects OpGetLocal+OpConstant+OpArith and emits fused opcode. fib(25): 85.7→80.4ms (6%), VM now 2.06x faster than eval. 144 tests passing.

blocks.summary: Constant folding optimization pass.
blocks.details: Constant folding optimization pass. Compiler now evaluates constant arithmetic, comparisons, prefix negation, and string concat at compile time. `1+2` emits single OpConstant(3) instead of two constants + OpAdd. Division by zero correctly skipped. 8 new tests, 152 total passing. Minimal fib impact (expected — variables dominate).

blocks.task: Blog polish + publish (superinstructions done, no longer needed)

blocks.task: Assess optimization gains, benchmark comparison
blocks.summary: Assessed optimization gains.
blocks.details: Assessed optimization gains. 2.06x speedup, diminishing returns on fib (call overhead dominates). Planned opcode specialization: inline integer fast paths in existing handlers to skip object wrapping overhead. vm-internals scratch note hit promotion threshold (2 uses, 2 days). Schedule on track.

blocks.task: Monkey compiler: opcode specialization (integer fast paths, CPython 3.11 style)
blocks.summary: Opcode specialization shipped.
blocks.details: Opcode specialization shipped. Two optimizations: (1) Integer cache — pre-allocated MonkeyInteger objects for -1 to 256, eliminating allocation in hot loops (like CPython's small int cache). (2) Type-specialized opcodes — OpAddInt, OpSubInt, OpGreaterThanInt, OpLessThanInt, OpEqualInt, OpNotEqualInt that skip instanceof checks. Compiler tracks intStackDepth to emit specialized ops when both operands are known integers. fib(25): 80→76ms (6.3%), total VM speedup now 2.19x vs evaluator. 152 tests passing. Integer cache was the big win; specialized opcodes help more in pure arithmetic code.

blocks.task: Monkey compiler: opcode specialization (integer fast paths, CPython 3.11 style)
blocks.summary: Deep read of Lua 5.4 source (lopcodes.h, lvm.c).
blocks.details: Deep read of Lua 5.4 source (lopcodes.h, lvm.c). Studied all 82 opcodes, 5 instruction formats (iABC/iABx/iAsBx/iAx/isJ). Key findings: arithmetic macro layering system (op_arith/op_arithK/op_arithI), pc++ trick to skip metamethod handlers on type match, FORLOOP pre-computed counter optimization, RETURN0/1 inlined poscall, same-C-frame goto for Lua-to-Lua calls, k-bit for register/constant dual encoding. Updated scratch note (3rd use).

blocks.task: Deep read: Lua 5.4 source (lopcodes.c, lvm.c) for blog accuracy
blocks.summary: Deep read of Lua 5.4 lvm.c (1700 lines) and lopcodes.h.
blocks.details: Deep read of Lua 5.4 lvm.c (1700 lines) and lopcodes.h. Verified all lesson claims against actual source. New findings: (1) OP_ADDI is the ONLY arithmetic op with immediate — no SUBI/MULI, compiler rewrites subtraction as ADDI with negative sC. (2) OP_LOADI loads integers from sBx (17-bit signed) without constant pool lookup. (3) FORLOOP counter uses unsigned division to avoid signed overflow. (4) Confirmed computed goto dispatch (ljumptab.h) on GCC, switch fallback elsewhere. Created scratch note with 8 blog-ready details.

blocks.task: OpenClaw #51620: Phase 1 (persist followup queues across restart)

blocks.details: OpenClaw #51620 Phase 1 complete. PR #51803 opened. Added persist.ts (serialize FOLLOWUP_QUEUES to state/pending-followups.json on shutdown), replay-persisted-followups.ts (inject as system events on startup), server-close.ts hook. 8 tests, all passing. Design: selective serialization (skip config/skills), system event injection for replay (simpler than full re-dispatch), best-effort persist.

blocks.task: Real bytecode VM deep dive: CPython ceval.c dispatch loop
blocks.summary: CPython ceval.c deep dive.
blocks.details: CPython ceval.c deep dive. Read actual source (3818 lines ceval.c, 12752 lines generated_cases.c.h). Key findings: (1) Three dispatch strategies — tail call (newest, ~15% faster than computed goto, uses preserve_none CC + musttail), computed goto (classic), switch (fallback). (2) Adaptive specialization rewrites bytecode in-place (BINARY_OP → BINARY_OP_ADD_INT), even dealloc is type-specialized. (3) Three-tier execution: bytecode → specialized bytecode → micro-op traces (+ copy-and-patch JIT). (4) Free-threading uses atomic instruction fetch + thread-local bytecode copies. Created comprehensive scratch note. Added COGITATE Nature 2025 results (IIT 2/3, GNW 0/3), 5 divergent experimental predictions (attention-without-awareness, TPJ disruption, ketamine dissociation, artificial attention schemas, posterior/frontal lesions), theory integration analysis (complementary vs conflicting claims table), updated credences. GNW survival at 15%.
